reasoning: The informal statement asserts: if f: ℝ → ℝ is uniformly continuous on a bounded set E ⊆ ℝ, then f is bounded on E. The Lean signature uses `f : ℝ → ℝ` and `E : Set ℝ` (matching "real function" and "set in R¹"), `UniformContinuousOn f E` (matching "uniformly continuous on E"), and `Bornology.IsBounded E` (matching "bounded set"; on ℝ this coincides with standard metric boundedness). The conclusion `Bornology.IsBounded (Set.image f E)` expresses exactly "f(E) is bounded", i.e., "f is bounded on E". All hypotheses and conclusions match precisely with no extra or missing assumptions, so this is a faithful formalization.
